Source_Species.reason: The paper studies rice and transforms/edites Kitaake rice plants.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper consistently says rice but the Latin binomial Oryza sativa does not explicitly appear in the provided text.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Kitaake is explicitly stated as the rice background used for transformation and controls.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Rice is explicit, but Oryza sativa is not explicitly written in the provided paper text.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The CRISPR construct was transformed into Kitaake rice and compared to Kitaake controls.

Enzyme_Name.reason: CYP75B3 and CYP75B4 are explicitly described as genes encoding flavonoid 3'-hydroxylases.

EC_Number.reason: The EC number 1.14.14.82 does not appear in the paper text.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The CYP prefix implies cytochrome P450, but the paper does not explicitly state 'Cytochrome P450 family CYP75B' in the Results.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The work explicitly places these genes in rice flavone biosynthesis within the broader phenylpropanoid/flavonoid pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The paper states CYP75B3/CYP75B4 mediate conversion of apigenin to luteolin, matching the apigenin-to-luteolin branch.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The genes are part of a branch from apigenin to luteolin, but the exact phrase 'branch point' is not stated.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper explicitly says CYP75B3 and CYP75B4 mediate the conversion of apigenin to luteolin.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Apigenin is explicitly described as the substrate converted by CYP75B3/CYP75B4.

Primary_Product.reason: Luteolin is explicitly described as the product of the CYP75B3/CYP75B4-mediated conversion.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: It is inferable that this step competes with apigenin accumulation, but the paper does not use the specific label 'competes with target-product branch'.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Apigenin is the metabolite intentionally increased and linked to the observed downstream phenotypes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Apigenin is explicitly treated as a flavone/flavonoid in the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results show apigenin stimulates biofilm formation, promotes nitrogen-fixing activity, and edited plants show increased colonization-related biofilm signal, biological nitrogen fixation, and higher grain yield under low N.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Figure 3d,e and text explicitly state higher apigenin in root extracts and root exudates.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The double knockout lines with reduced CYP75B3/CYP75B4 transcripts had increased apigenin/apigenin O-glucoside in roots/exudates, increased bacterial biofilm induction, increased 15N assimilation, and higher seed yield under limiting nitrogen.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: qPCR was performed on leaf RNA and showed little transcript of both CYP75B3 and CYP75B4 in CRISPR lines.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The edited Kitaake lines showed little expression of targeted genes by qRT-PCR using leaf RNA.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: No subcellular localization such as ER/microsome is reported in the paper text.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The genes were selected as a practical control point to increase apigenin, but the paper does not explicitly establish them as rate-limiting.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described and used: CRISPR/Cas9 editing, sequencing, qRT-PCR, LC-MS/MS flavonoid profiling, biofilm assays, in vivo 15N2 incorporation, and yield analysis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: This accurately summarizes the reported double-knockout findings for CYP75B3 together with CYP75B4.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Low nitrogen conditions of 22.5 ppm N/30% nitrogen medium and both hydroponic and soil growth are explicitly described.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper reports 16S rRNA amplicon profiling and provides a figshare raw FASTQ link.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods explicitly mention TaU3 promoter and 35S terminator in the synthesized multiplex gRNA cassette.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: CYP75B4 was co-targeted with CYP75B3, but no protein-protein interaction is reported.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The Discussion and Summary explicitly support manipulating this pathway/these genes as a strategy to improve BNF and reduce fertilizer use in cereals.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Reduced plant height is shown in Results; Discussion notes altered flavone profile including decreased tricin and says impacts on pathogen resistance require further study.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper experimentally assesses the double knockout, not CYP75B3 alone, and links effects to altered root exudate flavone chemistry and biofilm-mediated BNF rather than direct nitrogen metabolism.

corrections.reason: The EC number 1.14.14.82 does not appear in the paper text.
No subcellular localization such as ER/microsome is reported in the paper text.
CYP75B4 was co-targeted with CYP75B3, but no protein-protein interaction is reported.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The gene is discussed in the rice flavone biosynthetic pathway within phenylpropanoid/flavonoid metabolism.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The paper explicitly places CYP75B4 in the apigenin-to-luteolin conversion branch.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The exact phrase 'branch point' is not used, even though the pathway role is consistent with a branching step.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results support apigenin as a stimulator of diazotroph biofilm formation and nitrogen fixation, with edited plants showing enhanced BNF and grain yield under low N.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Higher apigenin is explicitly reported in root extracts and root exudates.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The double knockout lines with reduced CYP75B4/CYP75B3 transcripts showed increased apigenin/apigenin O-glucoside, stronger biofilm induction, greater 15N assimilation, and increased seed yield under low nitrogen.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The listed methods are all reported in the paper for the double knockout analysis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: This is an accurate summary of the reported findings for CYP75B4 together with CYP75B3.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Low nitrogen conditions (22.5 ppm N; 30% nitrogen) and hydroponic/soil contexts are explicitly used.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods explicitly state TaU3 promoter and 35S terminator in the gRNA cassette.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: CYP75B3 was co-targeted, but no protein interaction between CYP75B4 and CYP75B3 is shown.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper explicitly proposes manipulation of the flavone pathway/these genes as a strategy to enhance BNF and reduce fertilizer demand.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Reduced height is observed in Results; Discussion states altered flavone profile including decreased tricin and notes possible disease-resistance consequences need study.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The study tested the double knockout rather than CYP75B4 alone, and effects are linked to altered root exudate chemistry and microbiome/biofilm-mediated BNF.
